channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-25
item.description:
Our open-source research community has been bustling with activity across multiple projects. Notably, the **scienceverse/metacheck** project saw updates to its main branch, streamlining navigation and documentation with changes made by Lakens. Meanwhile, **Davidvandijcke/coarse** made significant progress on both its 'main' and 'dev' branches, with improvements to localization, deployment, and review page functionality. Interestingly, these updates demonstrate a shared focus on enhancing user experience and accessibility across projects. Additionally, **JamieCummins/regcheck** made strides in improving multistudy ingestion and local LLM support on its staging branch, showcasing the community's commitment to innovative research tools. Overall, these connections and advancements highlight the collaborative spirit and dedication to progress within our community.

51 commits across 5 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: escicheck, auto-zcurve

Most active:

docpluck16 commits
regcheck15 commits
metacheck10 commits
coarse7 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: metacheck: 3 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The main branch was the focus of the recent work, with two specific changes made to the repository. The home page text was updated to point to the Metacheck Manual, as stated in the commit message "Point home page text to the Metacheck Manual". Additionally, the navigation bar was modified to replace the "Get Started/Reference/Articles" links with a single link to the Metacheck Manual, as per the commit message "Replace Get Started/Reference/Articles navbar with Metacheck Manual link". The changes were made by Lakens on June 25, 2026. No other branches were worked on during this time.

3 commit(s) today:

  • 32659a9 [main] Point home page text to the Metacheck Manual — Lakens
  • d6a7f12 [main] Replace Get Started/Reference/Articles navbar with Metacheck Manual link — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 3 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

Work was done on the 'main' branch, where a feature was added for tables, specifically an "RC-T char-level column-detection fallback for tight-kerned tables" as part of version 2.4.98, which is described as the foundation. The documentation was also updated on the 'main' branch, with the addition of "2026-06-25 run handoff + LEARNINGS + project lessons", although this is noted as being partial. Additionally, on the 'main' branch, a fix was made to the tooling, which included adding a "direction-aware check_app_pin_sync note" and incorporating "RC-T/RC-1 spec findings". No other branches were worked on in these commits. The changes are specific to the 'main' branch, with no other branches mentioned.

3 commit(s) today:

  • 521089a [main] feat(tables): RC-T char-level column-detection fallback for tight-kerned tables (v2.4.98, foundation) — Gilad Feldman
  • 976af30 [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-25 run handoff + LEARNINGS + project lessons (PARTIAL) — Gilad Feldman
  • 59cff5b [main] fix(tooling): direction-aware check_app_pin_sync note + RC-T/RC-1 spec findings — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of recent work, where the multistudy ingestion was improved. On the staging branch, a debug of the CLI report was performed by Jamie Cummins. Additionally, on the staging branch, further local LLM support was added in the CLI. The staging branch also received UI fixes, as part of the recent changes made by Jamie Cummins.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 31fa103 [staging] improve multistudy ingestion — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The main branch was the focus of the recent work, with three commits made to it. The most recent commit, made on June 25, "sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6)" as stated, which suggests that the repository was synchronized with the escicheckapp repository at the specified commit hash, bringing in changes from effectcheck version 0.6.6. On June 21, two commits were made to the main branch, one to "sync from escicheckapp@bd6f007 (effectcheck v0.6.5)" and another to "sync from escicheckapp@dec37b1 (effectcheck v0.6.4)", indicating that the repository was synchronized with the escicheckapp repository at those specific points, incorporating changes from effectcheck versions 0.6.5 and 0.6.4, respectively. No other branches were worked on during this period, with all changes being made to the main branch.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 244b2d8 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6) (#20) — Gilad Feldman

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →

item.title: auto-zcurve: activity update
item.description:
shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve

The `main` branch was worked on, where the validation note was revised and a citation was included, as stated in the commit message "Revise validation note and include citation". No other branches were modified in this set of commits. The specific change made was to the validation note, which was revised, and a citation was added to it. There were no additions or changes to any features, fixes, or modules other than the revision of the validation note. The commit message explicitly mentions the revision of the validation note and the inclusion of a citation, indicating that these were the primary focus of the change. No other work was done in this set of commits beyond the revision and addition of the citation in the `main` branch.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
